functional_purpose: Receives DICOM studies, extracts DICOM header and AE-title context, and submits the binary study to OpenEyes PayloadProcessor. Zeiss IOLMaster-compatible studies are also written to the IOLMasterImport incoming folder.
business_process: Ingest imaging and route supported biometry studies for clinical processing.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM listener auto-generates its response after the source transformer and responds after processing.
main_filters: No configured source or destination filter; IOLMaster destination removal is performed by response-transformer device and SOP-class checks.
main_transformer_stages: Read local and remote AE titles.
Derive identifier_type and institution_id from local AE title, falling back to configurationMap defaultPatientIdentifierType.
Classify source platform from AE titles.
Extract DICOM headers and remove the IOLMaster file destination unless the study is from a supported Zeiss IOLMaster.
error_handling: No configured destination retry or queue; the source keeps stored attachments and messages.
retry_behaviour: All destinations have queue disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer is 1000; destination queues are disabled.
logging: No explicit logger calls in enabled stages; development message storage and attachments are retained, creating a PII and imaging-data retention risk.

evidence.claim: Sussex DICOM (c14efd23-2c1c-4e53-a59f-dcfe0e727c3b) listens for DICOM on port 11112 and sends DICOM binary content to PayloadProcessor request/queue/add with request_type dicom_request.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/DICOM.xml sourceConnector DICOMReceiverProperties and destination PayloadProcessor API Send

evidence.claim: The channel reads configurationMap defaultPatientIdentifierType and only writes the IOLMaster file destination for supported Zeiss IOLMaster DICOM studies.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/DICOM.xml source transformer Extract identifier_type and institution_id from Local AET; getDicomHeaders response transformer remove first destination if type is not compatible

functional_purpose: Polls a document-management file drop for non-Internal binary files and transfers each file to an SFTP incoming path. Source files are deleted after processing and moved to the error directory on an error response.
business_process: Outbound delivery of clinical correspondence or documents to an external recipient.
main_filters: File Reader regex excludes filenames containing Internal.
main_transformer_stages: Transfer original raw data to the SFTP destination using the original filename.
error_handling: The reader moves files to /mnt/docman/error on error response.
retry_behaviour: SFTP destination has queue disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer is 1000; destination queue is disabled.
logging: No explicit logger calls; development message storage is enabled, creating a document-content retention risk.

evidence.claim: Sussex Document Delivery (375fe7b2-d55c-4c60-b66f-dc2000c8ea95) reads non-Internal files from /mnt/docman and deletes the source after processing, with error responses moved to /mnt/docman/error.
It sends message.rawData using the original filename to an SFTP destination with password authentication.

functional_purpose: Receives HL7v2 ADT messages over MLLP and maps patient, appointment, and patient-merge events to OpenEyes PASAPI HTTP operations. This migration listener uses TCP port 6662.
business_process: Migration-period synchronisation of PAS demographics, appointments, and merges into OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: TCP source auto-generates the HL7 response before processing.
main_filters: Appointment PUT accepts A01, A05, A08, and A11.
main_transformer_stages: Normalise double quotes in received HL7 XML.
XSLT-map patient content and extract hospital number, visit ID, dates, clinic, doctor, status, and NHS number.
Submit patient, appointment, and merge operations to PASAPI and parse response warnings and patient ID.
error_handling: Response transformers inspect PASAPI warning comments; no destination queue is configured.
retry_behaviour: All enabled HTTP destinations have queue disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer is 1000; destination queues are disabled.
logging: Enabled doctor-code mapping logs PD1.4.1, creating a potential clinician-identifier log risk; development message storage retains HL7 containing patient data.

evidence.claim: Sussex PAS IN - Migration (6b9477af-6106-41b7-bd2e-3273c1c9b2d3) has a TCP Listener on port 6662 and routes HL7-trigger-filtered messages to PASAPI V2 Patient and PatientAppointment and V1 PatientMerge endpoints.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/PAS IN - Migration.xml sourceConnector TcpReceiverProperties; HTTP destinations PASAPI - Patient, Clinic List - PUT/DELETE, and PASAPI - Patient Merge

evidence.claim: The migration workflow is structurally distinct from Sussex PAS IN because it listens on 6662 while PAS IN listens on 6661, and it has no disabled Nothing JavaScript Writer.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/PAS IN - Migration.xml sourceConnector and destinationConnectors; canonical/Sussex/PAS IN.xml sourceConnector and destination Nothing

functional_purpose: Receives HL7v2 ADT messages over MLLP and maps patient, appointment, and patient-merge events to OpenEyes PASAPI HTTP operations. The standard listener uses TCP port 6661 and additionally contains a disabled Nothing writer.
business_process: Routine synchronisation of PAS demographics, appointments, and patient merges into OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: TCP source auto-generates the HL7 response before processing.
main_filters: Appointment PUT accepts A01, A05, A08, and A11.
main_transformer_stages: Normalise double quotes in received HL7 XML.
XSLT-map patient content and extract hospital number, visit ID, dates, clinic, doctor, status, and NHS number.
Submit patient, appointment, and merge operations to PASAPI and parse response warnings and patient ID.
error_handling: Response transformers inspect PASAPI warning comments; no destination queue is configured.
retry_behaviour: All enabled HTTP destinations have queue disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer is 1000; destination queues are disabled.
logging: Enabled doctor-code mapping logs PD1.4.1, creating a potential clinician-identifier log risk; development message storage retains HL7 containing patient data.

evidence.claim: Sussex PAS IN (d69815ee-68d1-4dcb-8896-8b12ba06a9c4) has a TCP Listener on port 6661 and routes HL7-trigger-filtered messages to PASAPI V2 Patient and PatientAppointment and V1 PatientMerge endpoints.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/PAS IN.xml sourceConnector TcpReceiverProperties; HTTP destinations PASAPI - Patient, Clinic List - PUT/DELETE, and PASAPI - Patient Merge

evidence.claim: PAS IN has an additional disabled Nothing JavaScript Writer while PAS IN - Migration does not, and their TCP listener ports are 6661 and 6662 respectively.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/PAS IN.xml destination Nothing and sourceConnector; canonical/Sussex/PAS IN - Migration.xml destinationConnectors and sourceConnector

functional_purpose: Accepts an HTTP hosnum query, builds an HL7 QBP^Q21 demographic query, sends it to a remote PAS over MLLP, and transforms the returned K21 patient data into an HTTP XML PatientList response.
business_process: On-demand patient demographic lookup from a remote PAS.
ack_behaviour: The TCP sender validates the response and waits up to 60 seconds; the HTTP listener returns the XSLT-transformed response after a successful TCP send.
main_filters: TCP query sender rejects requests without QPD.3.1 search value.
K21 conversion runs only when Send Q21 has status SENT.
main_transformer_stages: Set query timestamp and message ID.
Map HTTP hosnum parameter to QPD.3.1.
Send QBP^Q21 to remote PAS and parse its K21 response as HL7 XML.
XSLT-transform PID records into an XML PatientList HTTP response.
error_handling: The K21 conversion is skipped unless the TCP sender status is SENT; no destination queue is configured.
retry_behaviour: All destinations have queue disabled and retryCount 0; the MLLP transport has maxRetries 2.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer is 1000; destination queues are disabled.
logging: The source transformer logs incoming HTTP Parameters, which can contain the requested hospital number; development message storage also retains request and response data.

evidence.claim: Sussex PAS OUT (06f0b8b8-5ea0-41a4-a0bc-04b89fb5193c) listens on HTTP port 6663, maps the hosnum parameter to QPD.3.1, and sends an HL7 query over MLLP to a TCP sender on port 43442.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/PAS OUT.xml sourceConnector HTTP Receiver and transformer Set hosnum parameter; destination Send Q21 TcpDispatcherProperties and filter

evidence.claim: The response workflow parses Send Q21's returned HL7 message and XSLT-transforms PID data into PatientList XML returned by Create response.
evidence.location: canonical/Sussex/PAS OUT.xml destination Convert K21 to XML script and destination Create response XSLT Transform Response
